mnemonics: PURE & MIXED (for Nash Equilibrium types): Picture a PUREbred horse in a MIXED-up stable to remember Pure and Mixed Strategy Nash Equilibria.

SPNE (for Subgame Perfection): Think 'Seriously Perfect, No Errors' to remember that a Subgame Perfect Nash Equilibrium (SPNE) requires the strategy to be optimal in every subgame, not just the overall game.
P-O-S (for Price of Stability/Anarchy): Remember 'Performance Of System' - Price of Stability is the best possible equilibrium performance, Price of Anarchy is the worst.
keyPoints: Nash Equilibrium: A stable state where no player can benefit by unilaterally changing their strategy.
Subgame Perfect Nash Equilibrium (SPNE): A refinement of Nash Equilibrium that requires strategies to constitute a Nash Equilibrium in every subgame of the original game.

Price of Anarchy (PoA) & Price of Stability (PoS): Measures of how the efficiency of a system degrades due to selfish behavior of its agents; PoA compares the worst equilibrium to the social optimum, while PoS compares the best equilibrium to the optimum.
commonMistakes: Assuming all Nash Equilibria are Subgame Perfect. A Nash Equilibrium found through backward induction (SPNE) is always a Nash Equilibrium, but not all Nash Equilibria are subgame perfect.
Confusing Price of Anarchy with Price of Stability. PoA is a measure of the worst-case efficiency loss, while PoS measures the best-case possible with selfish players.
Believing that a player's strategy in a Nash Equilibrium must be their absolute best payoff overall. It only means it's their best response to the other players' current strategies.
